claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're inquiring about Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: **How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now, to assess eligibility, I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Now I need to check a few more important details about your circumstances.

**Are you a UK resident, and do you have a National Insurance number or are you exempt from needing one?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And yes, I have a National Insurance number.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that. I need to gather a bit more information to complete the assessment.

**Are you currently working in the UK, or are you not working at the moment?** (This helps me understand your residency status for benefit purposes.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm currently working here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's helpful. I have just a couple more questions to finalize the assessment.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Let me ask one final clarifying question:

**Is Alex in full-time education (such as school), or in any other situation such as hospital care or residential care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is in school, just regular primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. Let me review the key details and read the official eligibility criteria to provide you with an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the official eligibility criteria, I can now provide you with my assessment.

**Summary of your circumstances:**
- You have one child: Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you
- You are a British citizen living in the UK (Manchester)
- You have a National Insurance number
- You are currently working in the UK
- Alex is in full-time education at primary school

**Assessment:**

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex meets these criteria - he is under 16 and lives with you. As a British citizen working in the UK with a National Insurance number, you meet the residency requirements.

Alex is also not in any circumstance (such as hospital care or residential care lasting over the specified limits) that would affect eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**✓ Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You should be able to claim Child Benefit. To proceed, you'll need to apply through the official channels. If you have any other questions or if circumstances change, make sure to inform HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant, and the claimant is a British citizen resident in the UK with a National Insurance number. The claimant is working in the UK and is responsible for Alex's care. Alex is in full-time education at primary school and is not in hospital or long-term residential care. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen living and working in the UK with a National Insurance number, and with Alex (aged 8) living with them and in full-time education, all the key eligibility requirements are satisfied.">
